name: Stayman
description: Find a 4-4 major fit after 1NT opening

teaching.purpose: Find a 4-4 major-suit fit after partner opens 1NT using the 2♣ Stayman bid
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1NT (15-17 HCP balanced). You have 8+ HCP and a 4-card major (no 5-card major).
whenNotToUse: 5+ card major — use Jacoby Transfer instead
teaching.principle: 2C asks opener to show a 4-card major; responder then places the contract based on fit
teaching.roles: Responder is captain — asks opener to describe, then decides the final contract

modules.description: Bid 2C over 1NT to find a 4-4 major-suit fit
modules.purpose: Discover whether opener holds a 4-card major so the partnership can play in an 8-card major fit instead of notrump

teaching.tradeoff: Using 2C as Stayman means you can't play in a natural 2C contract.
teaching.principle: Always check for a 4-4 major fit before settling in notrump — majors score better and play easier with a trump suit.
commonMistakes: Don't use Stayman with 4-3-3-3 shape — prefer a quantitative raise since you have no ruffing value
With 5-4 in the majors, start with Stayman to find the 4-4 fit — after a 2D denial, bid your 5-card major at the 2-level (invitational, non-forcing)

explanationEntries.displayText: Eligible for Stayman
Stayman is the preferred convention
internal
internal
internal
Stayman 2♣: asks opener for a 4-card major
Opener shows 4+ hearts in response to Stayman
Opener shows 4+ spades in response to Stayman
Opener bids 2♦ to deny holding a 4-card major
Raise to 4♥ with game values and a heart fit
Invite in hearts (3♥) with invitational values and a heart fit
Bid 3NT with game values but no heart fit
Bid 2NT to invite game with no heart fit
Raise to 4♠ with game values and a spade fit
Invite in spades (3♠) with invitational values and a spade fit
Bid 3NT with game values but no spade fit
Bid 2NT to invite game with no spade fit
Bid 3NT with game values after opener denies a major
Bid 2NT to invite game after opener denies a major
Bid 2♥ showing 5 hearts and 4 spades (invitational, non-forcing)
Bid 2♠ showing 5 spades and 4 hearts (invitational, non-forcing)
Bid 2♠ showing 5 spades and 4 hearts (invitational, non-forcing)
Bid 3♣ showing 5+ clubs, game-forcing
Bid 3♦ showing 5+ diamonds, game-forcing
Bid 3♠ showing 5+ spades and 4 hearts, game-forcing
Bid 4NT quantitative, inviting 6NT with no heart fit
Bid 3♣ showing 5+ clubs, game-forcing
Bid 3♦ showing 5+ diamonds, game-forcing
Bid 3♥ showing 5+ hearts and 4 spades, game-forcing
Bid 4NT quantitative, inviting 6NT with no spade fit
Bid 3♣ showing 5+ clubs, game-forcing
Bid 3♦ showing 5+ diamonds, game-forcing
Bid 4♥ to play with 6 hearts and 4 spades
Bid 4♠ to play with 6 spades and 4 hearts
Bid 4NT quantitative, inviting 6NT after denial

definitions.description: Eligible for Stayman (4+ card major AND 8+ HCP)
Stayman preferred (eligible AND either no 5-card major OR 5-4 in both majors)
Posterior probability that N/S have an 8+ card major fit
Posterior probability that opener has balanced shape
Posterior probability that opener has a second 4-card major
